assertions.description: The @function at-rule is recognised by the CSS parser as a way to declare a custom function.
A custom function (prefixed with --) can be called inside a property value; CSS.supports parses the function call shape.
@property registers typed custom properties — the typing layer custom functions reuse for their parameter and return types.
CSS.registerProperty is exposed so authors can register the typed custom properties their custom functions accept.
var() with a fallback continues to parse — custom functions are defined in terms of var() and parameter resolution.
Injecting `@function --double(--x) { result: calc(var(--x) * 2); }` into a constructible stylesheet should not be rejected by the parser when @function is supported.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const s=new CSSStyleSheet();s.replaceSync('@function --double(--x) { result: calc(var(--x) * 2); }');return s.cssRules.length>0;}catch(e){return false;}})()
